The variation also made the banning order subject to a condition that at the end of the period of prohibition Ms Kaur is restricted for a period of 12 months from 5:00 pm on 15 April 2026 from engaging in the provision of care and service to aged care consumers unless she is under direct supervision. For the purposes of the condition, “direct supervision” means that a supervisor is physically present at all times to observe Ms Kaur’s practice while providing care and services to aged care consumers in any aged care setting. On 7 January 2026 the banning order made against Ms Kaur was varied pursuant section 505(3) of the Aged Care Act 2024 to prohibit her from being involved in the delivery of funded aged care services generally for a period of 3 years, with effect from 5:00 pm on 15 July 2024. The variation also made the banning order subject to the following 2 concurrent conditions that apply to Ms Kaur at the end of the period of prohibition, from 5:00 pm on 15 July 2027: 1) subject to condition 2, Ms Kaur must not engage in the delivery of funded aged care services generally to an individual accessing funded aged care services unless she is under direct supervision for a period of 24 months. 2) For the first 6 months of the supervision period, Ms Kaur must not engage in the delivery of funded aged care services generally to an individual accessing funded aged care services who has been assessed as living with cognitive impairment including dementia. In this context “direct supervision” means that a supervisor is physically present at all times to observe Ms Kaur whilst she delivers funded aged care services to an individual accessing funded aged care services.

On 18 October 2024 a banning order was made under section 74GB(1) of the Aged Care Quality and Safety Commission Act 2018 that prohibits Axel Tunggal Ismail Roberts from being involved in the provision of any type of aged care until he meets the requirements set out below. The banning order order will remain in force until 14 days after Mr Roberts has provided satisfactory evidence in writing to the Commissioner that he has undertaken and successfully completed the following Australian Government recognised courses, or equivalents: - CHCAGE013 – Work effectively in aged care; and - HLTHPS006 – Assist clients with medication. For the purposes of this banning order “satisfactory evidence” of having undertaken and successfully completed the required courses means a transcript or certificate or other written record prepared by the Registered Training Organisation (RTO) that identifies the full name, address and registration number of the RTO that conducted the training; the courses undertaken; the date the courses were completed; the outcome of the course (for example – pass or specific academic grade such as Credit or Distinction); and the name and position title of the person issuing or authorising the transcript, certificate or other record. The banning order takes effect at 5:00 pm (WA time) on 25 October 2024.
